Predeclared batch checks
Displayed-value rule: Every dollar figure in the cards and charts is the full modeled savings before any commercial allocation. A future verified-savings agreement may divide that amount between the customer and Glyph Systems. This report does not subtract either party's share.
Evidence boundary: This report measures modeled operational-loss avoidance in a controlled matched recovery harness using a conservative small-payment-processor economic profile. It is not realized customer revenue and does not represent a named company's private data. No market prediction, trading, fee selection, account access, or electrical-energy claim is included.

Executive result

Program 32 Full processed the same matched workload and physical capacity as the Strong controller across 72 complete independent ten-minute rounds. The cumulative full modeled savings were $2,907.50, with an aggregate full modeled savings rate of $4.0382 per minute.

The complete-round distribution had a median of $1.0593/min, a 10th percentile of $0.1191/min, and a worst round of $0.0641/min.

Methodology and fairness

Each complete round lasted ten minutes: twenty seconds of clean Anchor calibration, randomized active distortion and recovery, and a one-minute final clean drain. Profile order was balanced in randomized blocks so every four rounds contained LOW, MODERATE, HIGH, and BURST_SATURATION once.

All five controllers received identical transfers, hidden service outcomes, fault schedule, physical service ceiling, queue limit, retry limit, idempotency capability, visible state source, restoration timing, and frozen cost coefficients. Controllers differed only in scheduling, holding, verification, retry timing, quarantine, memory, and recovery interpretation.

The engine and workload logic are unchanged from Program 35. Program 36 changes the economic scale to a conservative small-payment-processor profile and displays full modeled savings instead of a pre-split Glyph share.

The persistent-memory track carried only clean Anchor and smoothed memory estimates between rounds. Transactions, queues, active distress, tension, residue, Stillness state, and release state reset every round.

Frozen small-processor accounting
